title: Ambient Diffusion Policy: Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Data in Robotics
summary: Ambient Diffusion Policy leverages suboptimal demonstrations alongside high-quality data in robot imitation learning via noise-dependent data usage. It restricts when suboptimal samples contribute to training — using them at low-noise locality windows and high-noise contraction windows where their features align with the target task. The approach exploits the observation that diffusion policies learn task-level structure at high noise levels and refine motion primitives at low noise levels, enabling selective use of mixed-quality datasets via a simple change to the Diffusion Policy dataloader.
